description: Minimal headed-emulator + autoRecord permutation. A `device.headless: false` context asks Doc Detective to boot the Android emulator with a visible window, and `autoRecord` (test-level) injects a synthetic full-context ffmpeg screen capture around the steps. autoRecord fires because the context opens a VISUAL surface — the native app surface (`startSurface` with an `app`), not just a browser step — so the emulator window on the host display is captured. The `device` omits a `name`, so the default provisioned `doc-detective` AVD is reused (booted, not created) — no runtime avdmanager/Java needed. On a capable host (virtualization enabled, SDK/system image installed) this boots the headed emulator and records the run; on every incapable host (no KVM/HVF/WHPX, or no SDK — e.g. headless CI) the android preflight lands the context SKIPPED, never FAIL.

tests.description: Open the preinstalled Settings app on a headed emulator, wait for it to become ready, and close the surface. autoRecord wraps these steps in a synthetic ffmpeg recording of the host display.
